metadata.title: Configure SQLite Database with Entity Framework Core

story.iWant: Entity Framework Core configured with SQLite provider
story.soThat: I have a robust ORM for local data persistence
story.tasks: - Install required NuGet packages (Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite 9.0.12, Design, Tools to main project; Sqlite to test project) - Create Data/CalendarDbContext.cs inheriting DbContext, constructor accepting DbContextOptions, OnModelCreating placeholder, WAL mode + FK enforcement via PRAGMA - Configure database path: resolve %LOCALAPPDATA%\GoogleCalendarManagement\ at runtime in App.xaml.cs, create directory if missing, store in DatabaseOptions class - Register CalendarDbContext with DI via services.AddDbContext in App.xaml.cs ConfigureServices, pass computed connection string - Apply initial migration on startup: create InitialCreate migration, call dbContext.Database.MigrateAsync() during app launch - Write integration tests in Tests/Integration/DatabaseInfrastructureTests.cs: CanBeInstantiated, CreatesFileAtExpectedPath, BasicCrudOperations, MigrationApplied - Validate: build Debug+Release, launch app and verify calendar.db at AppData path, inspect WAL files, confirm __EFMigrationsHistory, run all tests

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given the .NET 9 WinUI 3 project from Story 1.1 When I configure Entity Framework Core with SQLite Then the application creates and connects to a SQLite database on first launch

The DbContext is properly configured: - CalendarDbContext class created with dependency injection registration - Database file stored in %LOCALAPPDATA%\GoogleCalendarManagement\calendar.db - Connection string managed via configuration (not hardcoded) - EF Core migrations system enabled

Database infrastructure is testable: - CalendarDbContext can be instantiated in unit tests using in-memory SQLite - Database file is created at the expected location on first launch - Basic CRUD round-trip operations succeed - Database schema version is tracked via EF Core migrations (__EFMigrationsHistory table)

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: SQLite (embedded, file-based). Entity Framework Core 9.0.12. Code-First migrations. Location: {AppData}/GoogleCalendarManagement/calendar.db. 14 tables in full schema.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite Version="9.0.12", Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Tools Version="9.0.12", Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design Version="9.0.12"

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: services.AddDbContext<CalendarDbContext>(options => options.UseSqlite("Data Source=calendar.db")); Register as scoped (default for AddDbContext).

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: ALWAYS dispose DbContext (use using or DI scoped). Preferred: inject via constructor. Scoped lifetime — disposed automatically at end of scope.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: UI (WinUI 3) → Core (Business Logic) → Data (EF Core) → Database (SQLite). Services: Integration tests with in-memory SQLite. Dependency rule: inner layers don't reference outer layers.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: WAL mode enabled for crash recovery (NFR-D1). Foreign key constraints enforced via PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON. Transaction safety required.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Full schema is 14 tables: gcal_event, gcal_event_version, date_state, tracked_gap, toggl_data, youtube_data, call_log_data, generated_event_source, weekly_state, save_state, audit_log, system_state, data_source_refresh, config. Story 1.2 establishes the DbContext only — Story 1.3 adds entities.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Install NuGet: Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite, Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design. Database path: %LOCALAPPDATA%\GoogleCalendarManagement\calendar.db. Enable WAL mode for crash recovery (NFR-D1). Set up database factory pattern for testability. Reference: FR-8.1, NFR-D3.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.section: FR-8.1 Local Storage / NFR-D1 Crash Recovery / NFR-D3 Database Integrity
doc.snippet: FR-8.1: SQLite as local storage, database in user AppData. NFR-D1: WAL journal mode for crash recovery. NFR-D3: Foreign key constraints and referential integrity enforced.

doc.title: Database Schema Reference
doc.snippet: Reference document for all 14 table definitions. Story 1.2 does not implement tables — Story 1.3 adds the Phase 1 schema. Consult for column types, naming conventions (snake_case), and index patterns.

artifact.reason: Existing DI registration point. Story 1.2 adds AddDbContext and DatabaseOptions here. Pattern: ServiceCollection → BuildServiceProvider in OnLaunched.
App startup sequence where MigrateAsync() call must be added after DI setup and before window.Activate().
Existing test pattern. Integration tests for Story 1.2 should follow same xUnit + FluentAssertions style. New tests go in Tests/Integration/ folder.
Current dependencies. EF Core packages not yet added. Add Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite, .Tools, .Design v9.0.12 to this ItemGroup.
Test project dependencies. Add Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite v9.0.12 for in-memory SQLite test support.

package.name: Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite
package.note: Required for in-memory SQLite test factory

constraints.constraint: WAL journal mode MUST be enabled (NFR-D1). Set via SQLite connection string ("Mode=WALJournal") or PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL after connection open. Do not skip.
Foreign key enforcement MUST be set per-connection: PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON. SQLite resets this per-connection; it is NOT a persistent database setting.
CalendarDbContext must be registered as SCOPED (default for AddDbContext). Never singleton — causes threading and concurrency issues.
Connection string must NOT be hardcoded. Use a typed DatabaseOptions class populated from runtime path resolution. Path: %LOCALAPPDATA%\GoogleCalendarManagement\calendar.db
Directory %LOCALAPPDATA%\GoogleCalendarManagement\ must be created via Directory.CreateDirectory() before connection is first opened. App will crash otherwise.
EF Core CLI tools require Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design in the startup project. Migration commands: dotnet ef migrations add InitialCreate, dotnet ef database update.

Story 1.2 creates the DbContext and migration infrastructure ONLY. Entity/table definitions (the 14-table schema) are added in Story 1.3. Do not define DbSet properties for business entities in this story.
All code must use C# nullable reference types (Nullable enabled in .csproj). DbContextOptions parameter must be non-nullable.
Layer rule: CalendarDbContext lives in the Data/ folder of the main project (not a separate library) for this story. Future refactoring to GoogleCalendarManagement.Data project is deferred.
Integration tests MUST use in-memory SQLite (Data Source=:memory:) with OpenConnection() + EnsureCreated() — not the real AppData file — to remain fast and isolated.

interface.name: DatabaseOptions configuration class
interface.signature: public class DatabaseOptions { public string ConnectionString { get; init; } = ""; }

interface.name: Database startup migration
interface.path: App.xaml.cs — OnLaunched, after serviceProvider built, before window.Activate()

interface.name: In-memory test factory
interface.signature: var options = new DbContextOptionsBuilder<CalendarDbContext>().UseSqlite("Data Source=:memory:").Options; var ctx = new CalendarDbContext(options); ctx.Database.OpenConnection(); ctx.Database.EnsureCreated();

tests.standards: Integration tests use xUnit with FluentAssertions. Test project targets net9.0 (not windows-specific). Use in-memory SQLite (Data Source=:memory:) for all DB tests — never the real AppData file. Each test creates and disposes its own CalendarDbContext instance. Pattern matches existing SmokeTests.cs: [Fact] methods, Arrange/Act/Assert comments, FluentAssertions .Should() syntax. New tests live in GoogleCalendarManagement.Tests/Integration/ folder. No Moq needed for database infrastructure tests (real in-memory SQLite preferred over mocks for this layer, per architecture.md "Services: Integration tests with in-memory SQLite").

ideas.idea: CalendarDbContext_CanBeInstantiated_WithInMemorySqlite — create context with in-memory SQLite, assert not null, dispose cleanly
Database_CreatesFile_AtExpectedPath — resolve AppData path, call MigrateAsync, assert file exists at %LOCALAPPDATA%\GoogleCalendarManagement\calendar.db, cleanup after test
Database_CanExecute_BasicCrudOperations — insert a test record (use a minimal stub entity or raw SQL), read it back, delete it; assert round-trip succeeds
Database_MigrationApplied_SchemaVersionTracked — after EnsureCreated/MigrateAsync, query sqlite_master or __EFMigrationsHistory; assert InitialCreate migration entry exists
Database_WALMode_IsEnabled — open connection, execute "PRAGMA journal_mode;", assert result is "wal"
Database_ForeignKeys_AreEnforced — execute "PRAGMA foreign_keys;", assert result is 1
